slides.title: Measuring AI authorship of IETF drafts
Community Vitality through RIPE Mailing Lists

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I'm confused, is this a tool to facilitate and accelerate the existential threat to the IETF?

chat.author: Ryo Yanagida
chat.text: 'let's do some damage control' ? maybe?

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @mark are the iesg ballots and the iesg minutes both inputed.into the tool.

chat.author: Priyanka Sinha
chat.text:
Very positive talk for me .. I think the policy considerations would be addressed by better and larger LLMs mostly --
It would be awesome .. any further experimentation with a local LLM and say a open source LLM coding platform would be wonderful to try .. or even hosted by IESG/IETF/IRTF

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @mark - do AI tools aid sealioning?

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Like any tools it is used in all sorts of ways

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: Thanks for putting this all together, Mark.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: +thanks for creating the tools.

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: What a fine effort. thanks.

chat.author: Kaliya Young
chat.text: You could insist on proof of personhood - the Linux Foundation is moving this direction - https://lfms26.sched.com/event/2ETT5/facilitated-discussion-the-first-person-linux-kernel-maintainer-verification-project-greg-kroah-hartman-linux-kernel-maintainer-daniela-barbosa-hart-montgomery-lf-decentralized-trust-glenn-gore-affinidi

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: Maybe another thing - proof of thought: if you use AI, include your prompt and the input you gave it.

chat.author: Tom Newton
chat.text: If someone goes as far as getting Mark's tool, there's a degree to which that demonstrates engagement (and wanting to get things right)

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: Chairs: I suspect this is a really valuable conversation for the IETF so if you feel able to extend the timeslot a little that might be worthwhile

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: yes, we cut on the welcome to be able to do that

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: stilll, lots of questions :-)

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I agree people are using it (and we aren't going to be able to ban it), but I don't see how we are helping them by providing the tools to use it this way

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: if the norm becomes "don't ask questions, just ask Mark's LLM", that's bad for the users, and for the community, and gives a lot of power to the author of the tool

chat.author: Priyanka Sinha
chat.text: @nick the most visible impact I think with this kind of accuracy with the embeddings would be to accelerate the review backlog for IESG

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: @Nick, what would you envision would be the correct way for a participant to use AI? The answer helps directly inform formalized community guidelines

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Looks like we need blue tick marks in IETF - certified person?

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: @nick If you don't provide them, they're easy to replicate. If you provide them - you get control over what virtues those tools are imbued with.

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: @priyanka, in that the IESG should outsource their reviews to an LLM?

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: @alexandr control over the the virtues! interesting. I wonder if any attacker could change the instructions from "do disclose" to "don't disclose".

chat.author: Priyanka Sinha
chat.text: @nick they can use it as a more effective tool .. like we use calculators in accounting .. I work in AI accounting in my day job :-)

chat.author: Jean Queralt
chat.text:
The IETF should explore standardizing network prompting language.
I can't see how we will avoid people resolving their networking needs by prompting their swarms and go with it.

chat.author: Carolina Caeiro
chat.text: Am I beyond the lock, Ignacio?

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: @nick use remote attestation to ensure they're using the right tools ;)

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: in if you can be very succinct ;0

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: @priyanka, I guess I'm waiting for evidence that it improves reviews. my experience so far is that it does the opposite. misleads the reviewer, makes mistakes, encourages the reviewer to stop paying attention

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Things like Yang reviews for sure tools are good

chat.author: Priyanka Sinha
chat.text: @nick I agree to that

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: @jean that's a great idea

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: On using AI to comment on topics that you have no understanding, there seems to be a long tradition of people doing that anyway

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: +1 Andrew, a perfect example of AI only being new in its rate of production.

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: @Tommy, people do whatever they want in the absence of accountability

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: But who is actually reviewing all the -00 drafts?

chat.author: Jean Queralt
chat.text: @Lixia I did a HotRFC in 120 about "What does a post-IETF world look like" exploring the topic.

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Unless it is actuallly on the agenda

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: @tommy, I think translation and editing for communication clarity would be good uses.

chat.author: Jay Daley
chat.text: Requiring proof of being human won't really stop much - the most dangerous issue is real humans using AI to engage on topics they know nothing about

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: AI can help us analyze communications, expose everything on the table (yes this needs to be regulated / controlled to avoid negative impacts)

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: +1 jay, at least for now, the misuses are often approved by a human

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: @Jay, see my comment on accountability

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Many people also right drafts by borrowing styles from existing drafts and RFCs and modify it for your extensions for example! I wonder if those things would be flagged as AI written even if done manually?

chat.author: Alexandr Railean
chat.text: What does it show for drafts written 10..20 years ago?

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Jay, I agree... but real humans aggressively commenting about comments they know nothing about is already a serious enough problem --and one that have not dealt with well in the last couple of decades-- that I'm not sure how much worse AI involvement would make it.

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Perhaps, at worst, quantitative problem rather than a qualitative one.

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: @Dhruv  AI can learn to differentiate in anyway one wants

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Maybe we should tackle the behavior irrespective of it comes with or without Ai

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: yes

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: That might well be a better approach

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Lixia, I hear you. I don't have succinct thoughts for chat prior to proposal text, but I do hear you that rules without accountability aren't helpful

chat.author: avri doria
chat.text: AI's role in job generation: human in the loop verifiers.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Yes, exactly what Dhruv is saying. Overloading a list via AI or via unpaid sock puppet interns is the same problem.

chat.author: Andrew Campling
chat.text: +1 to tackling the behaviour.  As I said on the mic, reviewing everything to see where we assume good faith engagement and making adjustments would be a great place to start.

chat.author: Jay Daley
chat.text: @Lixia I agree on the need for visibility - see the tool I (Claude) wrote yesterday: https://github.com/JayDaley/mailing-list-ai-check

chat.author: Miracle Gerrald
chat.text:
AI should be used responsibly, and used as an accelerator and assistant. We cannot just replace and fully trust the AI to do the in-depth research for us. We have to re-check its validity, ensuring the response is reliable yet accountable.
For newcomers, I think one of the good practice for using AI, is being proactive with them and asking for a reference before sending the prompt/instructions. Therefore we can at least verify if the answers are legit or not.
Setting up an MCP server, with a custom instructions to see the provided resources first before searching the rest on the Internet, is also another good option to try.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: The other thing that comes to mind with this and the previous presentation: we need to avoid talking about AI as acting on behalf of anybody. The human bears the same responsibility for their behavior and content whether they do it, an LLM does it, or a non-LLM text generator does it

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: hm, rephrase: the human is the only actor. everything else is a non-participating tool.

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: yes human is the only responsible actor, must take responsibility for his/her AI tools actions

chat.author: Priyanka Sinha
chat.text: I wholly agree with you @Tommy .. and I have a granted patent that points towards this ideology. US9591087B2

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: Agreed, but I would even go so far as say only actor. What I'm thinking is if anything is acting on my behalf, then by definition I haven't seen the final form of content before it is submitted, which is an automatic failure in responsibility.

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: (have to run to conflict, thank you to both speakers and the great discussion)

chat.author: Lixia Zhang
chat.text: agree

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: Alvaro I suggest the AI interim would be worth publicising on ietf@ietf.org as it's likely to be of wide interest. (And personally speaking I'm not subscribed to rasprg.)

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: makes sense

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: To presenter do you track mortality and the IESG review state?

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: I-D to RFCs takes years so year on year correlations would not be there

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: indeed, and increased substantially over time (3 years median from 00 to RFC in recent years)

chat.author: Carolina Caeiro
chat.text: wonder how they estimated associated region for the proposals

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Be interesting to see variations on some of the graphs scaled by participants

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: RfC publication rated are impacted by IESG review cycle.

chat.author: MICHEL SALES BONFIM
chat.text: Ow, this tool really exists

chat.author: John Klensin
chat.text: Susan, of course.  And my comment about scaling applies there too -- many of these graphs and statistics might be more useful if scaled by various external factors -- IESG review cycles, regional participation, etc.

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: I don’t think here IESG state was not considered at all

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Not all drafts are meant to be RFcs

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3744200.3744757 on affiliations

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Maybe a new state in datatracker

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: If the charts are measuring the same thing (no. of emails in time interval) then IETF has about 30x more volume than RIPE

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: Beyond the population size in each org, the need for email communication varies, so would look at trends rather than absolute value

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: I was offering no opinion on which one was better. There are arguments for both.

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Older generation or just longer being on the list

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Need some correlation to understand at what age does one join a community

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I'm curious about the shift to other communication tools/fora

chat.author: Tommy Jensen
chat.text: +1 literally was just about to ask about GitHub stealing from email

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: we did some work on trying to track multiple email addresses in BigBang, around From addresses and names like that

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: But if you made it to IETF you are likely used to mailing list already even if not preferred

chat.author: Ignacio Castro
chat.text: Also did some mapping to unique identifiershttps://sodestream.github.io/paper-the-web-we-weave-untangling-the-social-graph-of-the-ietf.html

chat.author: Tom Newton
chat.text: I remember posting "mailing lists are important and fun" was the first task we were given as undergrads (late 90s Cs course)

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: The way slide 3 was drawn tends to imply (to me at least) that implementations occur after the standard is developed. As we know, running code is frequently an integral part of the development process. I haven't read the draft so I don't know if the draft is clearer on that point?

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I would love to see that Huawei slide

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I was surprised by how confusing it was even with things like Cisco, and different segments or representations of Cisco

chat.author: Ryo Yanagida
chat.text:
@Nick Doty  https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/interim-2026-rasprg-01/materials/slides-interim-2026-rasprg-01-sessa-analysing-internet-standards-development-organisation-data-00
Slide 7

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: :clap:

chat.author: Ryo Yanagida
chat.text: loads of fun

chat.author: Chris Box
chat.text: Sounds like there is value in starting to routinely collect affiliation data. Would also allow proof of humanity.

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I think if the authors are willing to work with rasprg, I think it would be great for rasprg to adopt and eventually publish this

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: lack of datatracker documentation is a really important finding and concrete recommendation

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: thanks all for excellent presentations!
